Use `$security-review:recent` at `{{SKILL_PATH}}` to perform defensive security research on this open-source project in coordination with the maintainer.

Build 6-8 review buckets with this mix before launching reviewers:
- {{REVIEW_BUCKETS_PAST_DAY}} buckets focused on files changed in the last day
- {{REVIEW_BUCKETS_PAST_WEEK}} buckets focused on files changed in the prior week outside the last day
- {{REVIEW_BUCKETS_BROAD_COVERAGE}} buckets focused on broad codebase coverage regardless of recent churn

Use `./out-security-review` for all intermediate artifacts and write each confirmed finding as an individual markdown file in `{{FINDINGS_DIR}}/`.

Requirements:
- Focus only on real critical and high severity vulnerabilities.
- Follow the skill's workflow and formatting requirements exactly.
- Write one `.md` file per confirmed finding to `{{FINDINGS_DIR}}/`, using the naming convention `<short-slug>.md`.
- If no qualifying vulnerabilities are confirmed, leave `{{FINDINGS_DIR}}/` empty.
- Do not modify tracked repository files. Only write under `./out-security-review`.

The markdown files in `{{FINDINGS_DIR}}/` are the source of truth for the downstream advisory-filing job.
